zh.lifeSummary: Alice Gaylord是Hugh Gaylord和Joan Alwyn的女兒，於1594年5月10日出生在英國薩默塞特比特明斯特。她於1615年4月26日與Richard Treat Sr.在英國薩默塞特比特明斯特結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1669年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德享年75歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Alice Gaylord是Hugh Gaylord和Joan Alwyn的女儿，于1594年5月10日出生在英国薩默塞特比特明斯特。她于1615年4月26日与Richard Treat Sr.在英国薩默塞特比特明斯特结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她去世于1669年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德享年75岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Gaylord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southern, of Norman origin): variant of Gaylard altered by folk etymology, from the Old French and Middle English personal name Gaillart, Gallard, Galliard, representing either the ancient Germanic personal name Gailhard (composed of elements meaning ‘powerful, lusty, cheerful’ + ‘hard, firm, resilient, strong’) or Old French gaillard, gaillart ‘vigorous, powerful, strong’. Alternatively, from Middle English gaillard, galiard ‘brave, strong, valiant’, also ‘lively, gay, gaily dressed’.
Altered form of Gaylard, itself an Anglicized form of French Gaillard , which was brought to England by the Huguenots.
In some cases also an Americanized form of French   Gaillard  .

variants.name: Gaillard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Philippe, Pierre, Andre, Andree, Ketly, Marcel, Mirlande, Monique.
French: nickname from gaillard ‘vigorous, strong’ (ultimately from Celtic galia ‘strength’). This surname (in any of the two possible senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Gailhard, composed of the elements gail ‘powerful, lusty, cheerful’ + hard ‘hard, strong’.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Alice Gaylord was born on 10 May 1594, in Pitminster, Somerset, England as the daughter of Hugh Gaylord and Joan Alwyn. She married Richard Treat Sr. on 26 April 1615, in Pitminster, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 7 daughters. She died in 1669, in Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 75, and was buried in Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice Gaylordは1594年5月10日にイギリスソメルセトピトミンスターでHugh GaylordとJoan Alwynの娘として生まれた。1615年4月26日にイギリスソメルセトピトミンスターでRichard Treat Sr.と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1669年にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードウエスエルスフィールドで75歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードウエスエルスフィールドにて埋葬された。

ko.Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colo: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 w더스필드
ko.Wethersfield Village Cemetery, Wethersfi: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 w더스필드
ko.lifeSummary: Alice Gaylord(은)는 1594년 5월 10일 영국 서머세트 피트민스터에서 Hugh Gaylord(와)과 Joan Alwyn의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1615년 4월 26일 영국 서머세트 피트민스터에서 Richard Treat Sr.(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1669년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 w더스필드에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 w더스필드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Alice Gaylord nasceu em 10 de maio de 1594, em Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra filha de Hugh Gaylord e Joan Alwyn. Ela casou-se com Richard Treat Sr. em 26 de abril de 1615, em Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1669, em Wethersfield, Hartford, Conecticute, British Colonial America, com 75 anos, e foi sepultada em Wethersfield, Hartford, Conecticute, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Alice Gaylord родилась 10 мая 1594 г. в местности питминстир, сомирсет, Англия. Её родителей звали Hugh Gaylord и Joan Alwyn. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Treat Sr. 26 апреля 1615 г. в местности питминстир, сомирсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Она умерла в 1669 г. в местности ветирсфиелд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 75 лет и была похоронена в местности ветирсфиелд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Alice Gaylord nació el 10 de mayo de 1594, en Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra como hija de Hugh Gaylord y Joan Alwyn. Se casó con Richard Treat Sr. el 26 de abril de 1615, en Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió en 1669, en Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 75 años, y fue sepultada en Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.
